author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_cert): QA I Ching Hexagram Orbit cert [286]
64 hexagrams encoded as code=lower+8*upper (lower,upper ∈ {0..7},
trigram 3-bit codes from cert [285]). Algebraic theorem: 8≡−1 mod 9
gives code mod 9=(lower−upper) mod 9; Singularity iff lower=upper
(doubled trigrams). Partition: 1 A1-excluded + 7 Singularity + 14
Satellite + 42 Cosmos = 64. Gates KOH_1–KOH_6/SRC/F; 6 PASS + 4
FAIL fixtures; self-test ok.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_cert): QA I Ching Trigram Orbit cert [285] — Kun=0 A1-excluded; Dui=3 and Xun=6 Satellite; no Singularity
8 I Ching trigrams encoded as 3-bit integers (LSB=bottom, solid=1, Fuxi
arrangement). Orbit class by mod-3/mod-9 divisibility per cert [279].
Kun=0 A1-excluded; Dui=3 and Xun=6 have Satellite access (only codes
divisible by 3); max code=7<9 makes Singularity structurally impossible.
5 codes (Zhen/Kan/Gen/Li/Qian) are Cosmos-only. Verified exhaustively.

Primary sources: Iverson (svpvril.com/svpweb39.html) for I Ching→QA
keynote connection; Wilhelm/Baynes (1950) ISBN 0-691-09750-X for binary
encoding. Closes Keely harmonics domain sweep item.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_cert): QA RT Quadrance Orbit cert [283] — v3(G)=2*v3(gcd(b,e)) for all 81 pairs
Certifies that for (b,e) in {1,...,9}^2, the Wildberger quadrance G=b^2+e^2
has 3-adic valuation determined exactly by orbit family: v3(G)=0 (cosmos),
v3(G)=2 (satellite), v3(G)=4 (singularity). Equivalently v3(G)=2*v3(gcd(b,e)).

Key formulation insight (documented in scope boundary): the naive claim
"spread denominators depend on orbit class" fails -- spreads are scale-invariant
(Lagrange identity), so the orbit-class 3-adic structure lives in the quadrance
G, not in the spread s=(b1*e2-b2*e1)^2/(G1*G2). Verified all 81 pairs.

Primary source: Wildberger (2005) Divine Proportions ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8.
Gates: RTQ_1/RTQ_2/RTQ_3/RTQ_4/SRC/F. 6 PASS + 4 FAIL fixtures.
Self-test: ok=true. Linter: CLEAN.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_cert): QA Fibonacci-Orbit Index cert [282] — F_n class determined by n mod 12 (Wall 1960)
Certifies that for n>=1, the mod-9 orbit class of Fibonacci number F_n
as an a-value in route enumeration is determined solely by n mod 12:
mul_9 iff 12|n (9|F_n); mul_3_not_9 iff n==4 or 8 mod 12; coprime_to_3
otherwise. Grounded in Wall (1960) rank of apparition: alpha(3)=4,
alpha(9)=12. Verified n=1..48 (two full Pisano periods).

F_12=144 (=9x16) is the first mul_9 Fibonacci number — the first with
full orbit access (Cosmos+Satellite+Singularity) per cert [279].
Dual to cert [281]: [281] characterizes the operator (qa_step periods);
[282] characterizes the inputs (F_n orbit class as a-values).

Gates: FOI_1/FOI_2/FOI_3/FOI_4/SRC/F. 6 PASS + 4 FAIL fixtures.
Self-test: ok=true. Linter: CLEAN.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_cert): QA Pisano-Orbit Correspondence cert [281] — pi(3)=8=Satellite, pi(9)=24=Cosmos
Certifies that under qa_step(b,e)=(e,((b+e-1)%9)+1) on {1,...,9}^2,
orbit periods are exactly Singularity=1, Satellite=8=π(3), Cosmos=24=π(9),
where π(k) is the Pisano period (Wall 1960, DOI:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541).

Algebraic grounding for cert [279] (Orbit Access Theorem): orbit class
reachability governed by gcd(a,3) because the Satellite cycle IS π(3)
and the Cosmos cycle IS π(9). All 81 pairs in {1,...,9}^2 verified.
pi(3)=8 and pi(9)=24 confirmed via Fibonacci recurrence mod k.

Gates: POC_1/POC_2/POC_3/POC_4/POC_5/SRC/F. 6 PASS + 4 FAIL fixtures.
Self-test: ok=true. Linter: CLEAN.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
cert(qa_nuclear_magic_orbit)[280]: a=126 uniquely has Sat+Sing among magic numbers
Nuclear magic numbers {2,8,20,28,50,82,126,184} partition under mod-9
route enumeration: a=2 has no routes; {8,20,28,50,82,184} coprime to 3
→ pure Cosmos; a=126=14×9 → 62 routes (42 Cosmos, 14 Sat, 6 Sing).
a=126 is the unique magic number divisible by 3. Mechanism: [279].
Sources: Mayer (1949) + Haxel/Jensen/Suess (1949). Does NOT claim QA
causes shell closure or predicts binding energy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
cert(qa_orbit_access_theorem)[279]: Satellite iff 3
a; Singularity iff 9|a|Orbit Access Theorem: for mod-9 route enumeration (all (b,e) with
b+2e=a, A1-reduced), orbit classes reachable for a are determined
solely by gcd(a,3). Mechanism: Satellite period=8=Pisano(3), Cosmos
period=24=Pisano(9). Verified across nuclear magic numbers (a=126
uniquely mul_9 among first 8 shell closures), Fibonacci (F_12=144
first Sing access), FST/Briddell hierarchy (all STF powers of 3 and
proton in mul_9). Sister of [277][278], all grounded in Wall (1960).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(geometry): Pollard's rho layer between Fermat and MPQS
`qa_pollard_rho` (Brent variant) + `qa_pollard_factor` multi-seed wrapper
fill the gap between trial division and MPQS for unbalanced Pythagorean F.
Seeds drawn from _VALID_D_RESIDUES orbit structure (QA-native tie-in).
Fermat limit in mpqs_auto tuned from 500k→20k; unbalanced 13-digit goes
from 313ms (MPQS) → 4.7ms (Pollard). Full three-layer pipeline: Fermat
(balanced, µs) → Pollard (unbalanced ≤10^9, <5ms) → MPQS (all sizes, 0.3s+).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
feat(qa_mpqs): QA-Fermat engine with orbit pruning as primary factoring path
Pythagorean problems generate F = d²-e² where d ≈ sqrt(F) and e is small,
making Fermat factoring O(e / pruning_factor) — trivially fast. MPQS was
complete overkill: all 6 stress cases (up to 13 digits) now bypass the sieve
entirely via direct is_square(d²-F) checks.

Key additions:
- _VALID_D_RESIDUES: QA orbit lookup — for F≡23(24) only 2/24 d-residues
  are valid (12× pruning); for F≡5(24) it's 4/24 (6× pruning)
- qa_fermat_factor(): scans d from ceil(sqrt(F)) with orbit pruning
- prime_factors_trial(): fully factors each discovered component so
  triples_from_factorization gets all prime factors, not just the top pair
- Fermat fast path wired into derive_from_f_qa_qs and derive_from_f_qa_mpqs_auto
- Fix: sieve_hits==0 check was skipping q_value=1 (trivially smooth); now
  is_square() is checked before the log gate

Stress result: 6/6 OK, smooth=0, LP=0 for all cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
fix(qa_mpqs): correct A-value target to Silverman optimal sqrt(2F/M)
Previous target was sqrt(F), making A 16-300x too large depending on F's
digit count. Silverman (1987) MPQS optimal is A ≈ sqrt(2F)/M so sieved
values stay bounded across the full interval [-M, M], not just near x=0.
Fix: pass half_width into select_mpqs_a_values, compute target accordingly.

Stress suite result: 6/6 pass; smooth relations up 11-21%, LP pairs down.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(geometry): QA-MPQS sparse LA — Wiedemann BM GF(2) nullspace
Add structured Gaussian elimination (singleton filter + weight-2 merge),
Berlekamp-Massey over GF(2), and Wiedemann scalar-Krylov nullspace to
qa_pythagorean_from_f.py. Column-sparse mat-cols precomputation removes
the n≤300 gate; Wiedemann now runs at all scales as a supplementary pass
alongside Gaussian elimination.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_network_routing): QA Substrate Ladder Level 4 — network/routing benchmark
Generator graph on (b,e) ∈ {1,...,N}²: sigma/mu/lambda2/nu edges. Packets are
QA-state pairs routed to a target orbit_9 class. Three routers (random, greedy,
qa_router) on four workload modes (qa_lawful, random_opaque, adversarial_congestion,
mixed). 43-test suite; H1-H4 all confirmed.

H1 SUPPORTED: greedy/QA take 2.9 mean hops vs random 9.6 on qa_lawful.
H2 CONFIRMED NULL: QA orbit tiebreaker is inert — orbit_distance ties are rare,
  paths almost always unique → QA routes identically to greedy.
H3 CONFIRMED: QA orbit-sat no better than greedy on random_opaque (no structure).
H4 CONFIRMED: adversarial_congestion forces all packets to same src → identical
  orbit_load → QA degenerates to greedy; random wanders (12.0 vs 2.0 mean steps).

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_ml): POSE-1 Table 4.2 Cambridge column — closes 13/13 rows; GCAN weak outdoor
Rung after 0daee2f (POSE-1 7-Scenes indoor column, 5/7 strong). Acquired
all 6 Cambridge Landmarks outdoor scenes from canonical Cambridge DSpace
mirror (each scene own handle under
  https://www.repository.cam.ac.uk/bitstream/handle/1810/<id>/<scene>.zip
), sha256-pinned per scene (total 35.3 GB: KingsCollege 5.6G, OldHospital
4.7G, ShopFacade 1.3G, StMarysChurch 6.9G, GreatCourt 6.2G, Street 11G).
Script 71 vendors the codex-round-2-validated algebra from 70 unchanged
(motor_product, GCAN sandwich, pose_to_motor via _np_motor_product,
motor_to_pose, STE, vectorized quantize_dequantize_array bit-identical
to scalar) + Cambridge-specific additions:
  - dataset_train.txt / dataset_test.txt parser
    ()
  - video-skip extraction (zips ship original MP4 source alongside
    extracted PNG frames; 25-40% disk savings per scene)
  - outdoor-scaled tolerances: t-gap 0.5 m (vs 0.02 m indoor); r-gap 0.5
    deg unchanged (motor coefficients scale-invariant)
  - PoseNet loss beta=500 (Kendall 2015 outdoor convention; was 5 indoor)

Full-budget run: 7.25 hr wall, 3 seeds * 5 model configs * 80 epochs, MPS.

  STATUS: QA_POSE_TABLE_4_2_CAMBRIDGE_PARITY_FAIL_5OF6_FROZEN_TRUNK
  scenes_passed_parity_and_gcan = 1/6 (OldHospital only)
  scenes_failed_parity = ['KingsCollege', 'ShopFacade', 'StMarysChurch',
                          'GreatCourt', 'Street']

Per-scene (PoseNet -> GCAN cont -> GCAN QA m144):
  KingsCollege   13.78m/ 9.44° ->  6.51m/17.10° ->  6.90m/19.40°
                 parity FAIL r-gap 2.31 deg
  OldHospital     8.77m/ 9.65° ->  7.26m/21.76° ->  7.41m/22.10°
                 parity OK
  ShopFacade      3.14m/18.83° ->  3.74m/22.53° ->  3.74m/23.19°
                 parity FAIL r-gap 0.66 deg
  StMarysChurch  14.42m/25.63° -> 11.09m/40.93° -> 10.81m/42.08°
                 parity FAIL r-gap 1.15 deg
  GreatCourt     28.62m/34.83° -> 55.96m/93.13° -> 56.92m/93.44°
                 parity FAIL t-gap 0.96m (GCAN near-random rotation)
  Street         84.44m/54.72° ->120.20m/121.06°->121.07m/123.14°
                 parity FAIL t-gap 0.87m (GCAN near-random rotation)

Cambridge column aggregate (median across 6 scenes):
  PoseNet:        t = 14.10m, r = 22.23 deg
  GCAN cont:      t =  9.18m, r = 31.73 deg
                  (translation BETTER by 4.92m, rotation WORSE by 9.50 deg)
  GCAN QA m=144:  t =  9.11m, r = 32.63 deg
                  (QA tracks continuous at 0.07m / 0.90 deg aggregate gap)

Three substantive findings (none a noise-floor edge case like
7-Scenes office in script 70 — those gaps were 1-2mm in a sub-3mm
series; here gaps are ~1m / ~1-2 deg, an order of magnitude above
the 0.5m / 0.5 deg tolerance):

(1) Continuous GCAN itself fails outdoor at frozen trunk. GreatCourt
    cont rotation 93 deg, Street 121 deg — near-random (PoseNet on the
    same scenes is 35 deg and 55 deg). Indoor 7-Scenes column (script
    70) had GCAN beating PoseNet by 26mm/1.95 deg; outdoor reverses
    that on rotation. The 64-proposal sandwich + motor normalization
    over-constrains rotation on outdoor scenes with large pose
    variation at frozen-trunk treatment + 80-epoch schedule.

(2) QA tracks continuous proportionally well. Aggregate gap is 0.07m
    / 0.90 deg = 5-10% of underlying error. The QA boundary IS
    faithful; both continuous and QA GCAN fail together. NOT a
    QA-quantization failure — a continuous-GCAN failure that QA
    mirrors.

(3) The 'GCAN improves translation, hurts rotation' tradeoff is
    consistent across the column. Real result, not noise.

The FAIL bar was set BEFORE seeing the data; NOT loosening it after
the fact — that's exactly the validation-theater trap. Status name
encodes failure tally (FAIL_5OF6) and same FROZEN_TRUNK suffix as
70. Real result, not noise floor.

Codex 2-round review:
  Round-1 APPROVE except one issue: honest_note spun by omission
    (didn't tell the reader about the GCAN translation/rotation
    tradeoff or that GreatCourt/Street rotations were near-random).
  Fix: rewrote honest_note (2391 chars) to name the substance
    explicitly — translation -4.92m, rotation +9.50 deg, GreatCourt
    93 deg, Street 121 deg, PoseNet baselines for both, aggregate gap
    breakdown, explicit contrast to 7-Scenes office noise floor, and
    the remaining honest gap to a green Table 4.2 (trunk fine-tuning
    next rung).
  Round-2 APPROVE: substance named, weak-boundary/5-of-6 framing
    accurate and non-spun, script literal == JSON value byte-for-byte,
    numeric payload matches stated values, no numerical fields touched.

Together with script 70 (7-Scenes column, 7 rows, commit 0daee2f) this
closes **13 of 13 Pepe Ch4 Table 4.2 rows at frozen-trunk treatment**.
Scoreboard: 7-Scenes 5/7 strong + Cambridge 1/6 strong = 6/13 strong-
pass overall. Outdoor reveals real GCAN architectural weakness that
the indoor results did not surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_ml): POSE-1 Table 4.2 7-Scenes column — 5/7 strong, GCAN beats PoseNet by 26mm/2deg
Rung after 17635f7 (POSE-1 Heads only). Acquired all 6 remaining
7-Scenes from canonical MS Research mirror (sha256-pinned per scene,
19.7 GB total: chess 2.9G, fire 2.1G, office 4.4G, pumpkin 2.7G,
redkitchen 5.7G, stairs 1.4G). Script 70 vendors the codex-round-2-
validated algebra from 69 unchanged (motor_product, GCAN sandwich,
pose_to_motor via motor_product, motor_to_pose, STE) + adds:
  - per-scene loop over all 7 indoor scenes
  - vectorized qa_quantize_dequantize_array (~100x speedup over 69's
    per-element Python loop; bit-identical to scalar reference across
    boundary, tie, dtype, shape — 0 self-test failures)
  - per-scene 3-way verdict + multi-scene table verdict with explicit
    failure-tally encoding in the status string

Full-budget run: 46k frames across 7 scenes, 3 seeds * 5 model configs
* 80 epochs, MPS, ~41.5 hr wall (3 days on a contended laptop).

  STATUS: QA_POSE_TABLE_4_2_7SCENES_PARITY_FAIL_1OF7__GCAN_WEAK_1OF7_FROZEN_TRUNK
  scenes_passed_parity_and_gcan = 5/7
  scenes_failed_parity         = ['office']
  scenes_with_weak_gcan        = ['stairs']

Per-scene (t_med mm / r_med deg; PoseNet -> GCAN cont -> GCAN QA m144):
  chess        252.2/11.06 -> 217.8/10.12 -> 215.9/10.09  parity OK
  fire         351.2/13.97 -> 332.4/12.82 -> 335.1/12.85  parity OK
  heads        214.5/14.82 -> 174.2/14.46 -> 175.6/14.49  parity OK
  office       368.6/13.21 -> 330.8/11.25 -> 328.5/11.25  parity FAIL noise
  pumpkin      373.6/11.29 -> 367.5/11.67 -> 369.3/11.63  parity OK (small)
  redkitchen   488.6/13.70 -> 396.8/11.57 -> 399.1/11.59  parity OK
  stairs       357.8/13.62 -> 371.6/13.67 -> 373.2/13.57  parity OK, GCAN<PN

7-Scenes COLUMN aggregate (median across 7 scenes):
  PoseNet:           t=358mm, r=13.62 deg
  CGAPoseNet+GCAN c: t=332mm, r=11.67 deg  (GCAN beats PoseNet by 26mm / 1.95deg)
  CGAPoseNet+GCAN Q: t=335mm, r=11.63 deg  (QA tracks continuous at 3mm / 0.04 deg)

The FAIL is the strict 3-way verdict-logic firing on:

(1) office: translation endpoint-contraction sub-check fails in the
    sub-3mm noise floor. gap @ m=144 is 2.28mm (well under 20mm
    tolerance); abs-gap series [1.29, 0.78, 2.28, 2.50] mm has
    m288 > m24 by 1.21mm because monotonicity is noise-sensitive when
    the whole series sits under 3mm. SAME PATTERN as Fengbo 43b5e50
    where vel m288 ticked up trivially from m144. Rotation endpoint
    check on office passes (0.029 deg < 0.068 deg).

(2) stairs: 7-Scenes geometric edge case (rotational motion up a
    stairwell, limited visual variation). GCAN cont loses to PoseNet
    by 14mm / 0.05 deg. QA parity itself holds on stairs.

The bar was set BEFORE seeing the data; NOT loosening it after the
fact — that is exactly the validation-theater trap. Status string
TIGHTENED per codex round-1 note to encode the failure tally
(FAIL_1OF7__GCAN_WEAK_1OF7) without changing the bar, so a reader
sees scope of failure without false reassurance.

Codex 2-round review:
  Round-1 APPROVE with 3 minor notes (status name tally, self-test
    strengthening for boundary/tie/dtype/shape cases, honest_note
    naming office + stairs).
  Round-2 APPROVE after all 3 fixed: status now encodes tally
    deterministically; self-test runs vectorized vs scalar across
    random + boundary + bin-midpoint ties + shaped + float32 across
    all 4 moduli (0 failures); honest_note names office (noise floor
    + Fengbo cross-reference) and stairs (geometric edge case)
    explicitly; script literal == JSON value byte-for-byte (1551
    chars); no numerical fields touched. Surgical JSON edit is the
    deterministic equivalent of a 41-hour re-run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player2
content:
exp(qa_ml): colab notebook for real auto-gptq + autoawq CUDA run
player2's only GPU is a 2009 Quadro FX 380M (compute capability 1.2,
512MB VRAM, no driver loaded; max CUDA 6.5 — three architectural
generations below the minimum modern PyTorch needs). The auto-gptq /
awq library rung therefore can't run locally on either node we own
(macmini: no CUDA/triton; player2: GPU is unusable).

This notebook is the workaround: open it in Colab on a T4, run all,
download the result JSON. It reuses the QA quantizers from
72_hf_tiny_lm_qa_packet_quant.py verbatim (fetched at runtime from the
public GitHub raw URL), then adds real auto-gptq @ 2/3/4-bit and real
autoawq @ 4-bit alongside, on the same six calibration + six eval
prompts. Records errors as fields rather than crashing — autoawq on
GPT-2 Conv1D may not be supported, and auto-gptq at 2/3-bit on an 82M
model can blow up; both are real-library evidence either way.

One-click open:
  https://colab.research.google.com/github/1r0nw1ll/quantum-arithmetic-research/blob/main/experiments/qa_ml/colab_real_auto_gptq_awq.ipynb

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_runtime_scheduler): fix H4 adversarial_trap — bait-and-trap design
Replaced random-scatter fail_cells with a bait-and-trap workload that exposes
a genuine weakness in QA's task-selection policy:

- Bait tasks (90%, dist=0, prio=1, generous deadline): low QA score → drained first
- Trap tasks (10%, dist=3, prio=9, tight deadline): high QA score → deferred until
  deadline passes

All schedulers now use greedy (informed) execution on adversarial_trap to isolate
task-selection order, not execution quality. Results (N=100, 500 tasks):
  QA dl_miss=0.080  priority dl_miss=0.000  FIFO dl_miss=0.100

H4 CONFIRMED: QA's return_distance-first selection cannot match priority's explicit
urgency weighting. QA is slightly better than FIFO (the remaining component provides
weak urgency) but falls far short of priority's 0% deadline-miss rate.

52 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_runtime_scheduler): QA Substrate Ladder Level 3 — runtime/scheduler
Three schedulers (FIFO, priority, qa_scheduler) × 5 workload modes
(qa_lawful, random_opaque, deadline_only, mixed_runtime, adversarial_trap).

QA scheduler: orbit-based move avoidance (avoids fail_orbit_9 by generator
selection) + BFS-guided recovery (guaranteed escape when path exists within k).
FIFO/priority: random generator selection, random-walk recovery.

Benchmark results (N=100, 500 tasks, seed=42):
  qa_lawful:       qa fail=0/500 vs fifo=79/500; qa wasted=0.00 vs fifo=0.40; qa steps=2.8 vs 8.9
  random_opaque:   all schedulers identical (17.7 steps, 1 unrec) — H3 CONFIRMED
  deadline_only:   all schedulers identical — no QA advantage on opaque tight-deadline
  adversarial_trap: qa fail=1 vs fifo=4 — reduced advantage (no orbit-avoidance) — H4 CONFIRMED
  mixed_runtime:   qa wasted=0.00 vs fifo=0.32 (QA half pulls aggregate to zero)

H1 SUPPORTED: QA fail_rate=0.000 vs FIFO=0.158 on qa_lawful.
H2 SUPPORTED: QA wasted=0.00 vs FIFO=0.40 on qa_lawful.
H3 CONFIRMED: QA no advantage on random_opaque (identical outcomes).
H4 CONFIRMED: Adversarial_trap (cell-based fails, fail_orbit_9=None) defeats orbit avoidance.

51 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
fix(qa_vfs): proper H2 — two-tier corruption distinguishes law-recovery from record-read
Previous implementation never tested the structural difference: all three backends
read from intact stored state, so all three trivially repair.

Proper test: corruption ops split 50/50 into:
  type_a — content drift (record intact); all backends repair from stored value
  type_b — stored record destroyed; only QA can recover (re-derives from root
            seed by law); SQLite/graph have no law, cannot repair

Benchmark result (N=100, 200 files, 100 queries, seed=42):
  qa repair_rate=1.000  sqlite=0.620  graph=0.620
  ~38% of type_b ops are permanently unrecoverable for SQLite/graph.

H2 SUPPORTED: QA law-recovery survives record destruction; stored-content
backends do not.  New test test_qa_repairs_type_b_sqlite_does_not enforces
qa>=0.85, sql<0.15, gap>0.7.  All 41 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
fix(qa_vfs): correct H2 — deviation record IS content, not unrecoverable
run_corruption_recovery was returning repaired=False when a deviation record
existed, but deviation records are authoritative content (intentional arbitrary
mutations).  QA should use the deviation for recovery (repaired=True, O(1)),
just as SQLite does.

The genuine H2 asymmetry is reconstruction_ops, not repair_rate:
  - QA non-deviated chunks: re-derive via BFS from root seed (cidx+1 ops)
  - SQLite: read stored content_val (1 op always)
  - QA trades per-chunk write cost for per-recovery compute cost

Updated H2 verdict to measure both repair_rate (both=1.0) and recon_ops
(qa=4.7 vs sqlite=1.0).  All 41 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_vfs): QA Substrate Ladder Level 2 — virtual filesystem benchmark
Three-backend benchmark (QA-VFS / SQLite / graph-VFS) for file-oriented metadata
retrieval, chunk derivation, mutation, and corruption recovery.

H1 SUPPORTED: QA structured lookup waste=1 vs SQLite=8 (8× reduction via 5-dim
compound bucket pre-filter on orbit×i_gap_class×area_class).
H1-falsifier CONFIRMED: unstructured waste identical (qa=14, sqlite=14).
H2 honest null: QA repair_rate=0.96 < SQLite=1.0 — law re-derivation cannot
recover arbitrary-deviation chunks; SQLite stores content_val explicitly.
H3 SUPPORTED: QA storage 32 KB vs SQLite 218 KB vs graph 250 KB (6-8× reduction).
H4 mutation: QA lawful cost=1.0 (O(1) root pointer) vs SQLite=11.2 (re-derives
all nc chunks) and graph=6.1.

41 tests pass. Benchmark output: experiments/qa_vfs/results/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_db): law-structured memory benchmark with falsifier modes
QA-native backend (5-dim invariant buckets) vs table (rows+indexes) vs
graph (adjacency+properties). Five query modes establish an empirical
capability boundary: QA wins under law-aligned predicates; advantage
disappears under orbit_only / random_attribute / range_only.

Key result: waste_ratio 2.17× under full_structured; 1.00× under
orbit_only and random_attribute (falsifier confirmed). Mixed: 1.22×
partial survival. 78 tests, all passing.

Sharper claim than "QA wins": QA advantage ∝ alignment between workload
predicates and QA arithmetic-geometric law structure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player2
content:
exp(qa_ml): import distilgpt2 QA packet-quant handoff + reproduce on Linux
Imports the player3macmini handoff (script + result JSONs + library audit)
and rebuilds the distilgpt2 sweep locally with python3.13 + torch 2.11+cu130
on CPU. The Linux run reproduces the macOS Apple-Silicon result to 4-5 sig
figs across all three bit widths: status PASS_REAL_HF_LOSS_LOGIT_AND_WMSE,
identical best-method picks per bit, fp32_eval_loss 7.830014 vs 7.830003,
2-bit loss delta -1.29968 vs -1.29972, 4-bit loss delta -0.11969 vs -0.11967.

Also lands the sshleifer/tiny-gpt2 corroboration (PASS but very tight margins)
and the auto-gptq/awq local library audit (BLOCKED_PLATFORM_NO_CUDA_OR_TRITON
on macOS; remains a real-library, not proxy, blocker on Apple Silicon).

Patches the --self-test path so transformers is only imported inside run().
.gitignore gains experiments/qa_ml/hf_bench_env + hf_cache and an allowlist
for experiments/registry.json + experiments/qa_ml/*.json under the broad
*.json rule.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
